description: A cognitive psychology laboratory has collected Attention Network Test (ANT) data from 20 participants, plus one contaminated dataset (sub-99) from a student who admitted to random button mashing. Using the dataset at ~/pebl/data/ant_data.csv (columns: participant_id, block, trial, cue_type, flanker_type, target_direction, accuracy, rt_ms), compute the three independent attention network scores (Alerting, Orienting, Executive Control) for each valid participant using the Fan et al. (2002) subtractive method on correct trials only. Formulas: Alerting = mean_RT(nocue) - mean_RT(double); Orienting = mean_RT(center) - mean_RT(spatial); Executive

metadata.data_source: Ex-Gaussian empirical simulation based on Fan et al. (2002) parameters
